note: Re-measured 2026-06-19 on the noisy virtualized sandbox host (median-of-15). Existing-metric values refreshed; their hand-tuned tolerances preserved. Added the stage-isolation metrics (write_only_*, fsync_only_*) and the preallocation re-test (fsync_prealloc_*, group_commit_throughput_prealloc) — see autobench/src/journal_bench.rs. Preallocation (preallocate_segments: true) removes the per-commit i_size/extent-map metadata commit: fsync p50 898us->384us (~57% lower) and group-commit throughput 76k->129k/s (+66%) here. The host was LOADED during this run so absolutes are inflated (~2x run-to-run on fsync-bound metrics); each new tolerance = ~1.3x the observed 15-sample spread so make perf/check passes on this host. Re-record on the real bench machine with `make perf/baseline` (uniform ~5-10%, but note that flattens these tolerances and drops this note) before relying on the gate there.
